items.question: How do I claim a payout?
items.answer: Click on the payout link you received via SMS or email. Enter your details and verify with the OTP sent to your phone. Once verified, choose your preferred withdrawal method.

items.question: How long does an EFT take?
items.answer: EFT withdrawals typically take 1–2 business days to reflect in your bank account, depending on your bank.

items.question: My payout link has expired. What do I do?
items.answer: Payout links expire after 30 days. Please contact the person or business that sent you the payout and ask them to reissue it.

items.question: Can I split my payout across multiple accounts?
items.answer: No, each payout can only be sent to one bank account or withdrawal method at a time.

items.question: What happens if I enter the wrong bank details?
items.answer: If the bank details are incorrect, the transfer will be rejected and the funds will be returned to your GiniPayout wallet within 2–3 business days. You can then retry with the correct details.

items.question: How do I verify my identity?
items.answer: You will be prompted to verify your identity during your first withdrawal. You'll need your South African ID number and a selfie for facial verification.

items.question: What do I do if my OTP doesn't arrive?
items.answer: Wait up to 2 minutes, then tap 'Resend OTP'. If it still doesn't arrive, check that your phone number is correct. If the problem persists, contact our support team.

items.question: Is my money safe?
items.answer: Yes. Your funds are held in a regulated account and protected in accordance with South African financial regulations.

items.question: Can I change my linked phone number?
items.answer: For security reasons, phone number changes must be verified by our support team. Please contact us via email or phone to request a change.

items.question: What should I do if I suspect fraud on my account?
items.answer: Contact us immediately on 0800 123 4567 (toll-free). Our team is available Monday–Friday, 8am–5pm SAST. You can also email support@ginipay.co.za with the subject 'Urgent: Fraud Report'.

items.question: Can I send money to other people?
items.answer: GiniPayout is a restricted wallet. You can only withdraw or spend your funds. To send money to others, upgrade to the full Gini app.

items.question: Where can I spend my GiniPayout funds?
items.answer: You can spend your funds at any merchant that accepts your chosen payment method, or withdraw to your bank account via EFT.

items.question: Does my wallet balance expire?
items.answer: Wallet balances are valid for 12 months from the date of the last transaction. After this period, dormant funds may be forfeited. You will be notified before this happens.

items.question: Is there a fee to withdraw?
items.answer: Standard EFT withdrawals are free. Some instant payment methods may carry a small processing fee, which will always be shown to you before you confirm.

items.question: What is the GiniPayout savings feature?
items.answer: The savings feature lets you set aside a portion of your wallet balance automatically. You choose a target savings balance and an optional monthly increase, and GiniPayout manages the rest.

items.question: How do I enable savings?
items.answer: Go to the Savings page from your home screen, enter your desired savings target and monthly increase amount, then tap 'Enable Savings'. Your plan will be activated immediately.

items.question: What is a savings target balance?
items.answer: Your savings target is the balance you want to maintain in your savings pot. For example, if you set R500 as your target, GiniPayout will work towards keeping R500 set aside from your wallet.

items.question: What is the monthly savings increase?
items.answer: The monthly increase automatically raises your savings target by a set amount each month. For example, if your target is R100 and your monthly increase is R2.50, your target will grow to R102.50 next month, then R105 the month after, and so on.

items.question: Can I change my savings target at any time?
items.answer: Yes. Open the Savings page, update your target balance or monthly increase, and tap 'Save Changes'. Your new settings take effect immediately.

items.question: Can I withdraw from my savings balance?
items.answer: Your savings balance is part of your overall wallet. You can withdraw it at any time, though doing so will reduce your progress towards your savings target.

items.question: What happens if I clear my savings plan?
items.answer: Clearing your savings plan removes your target and monthly increase settings. Your wallet balance is not affected — only the savings configuration is deleted. You can set up a new savings plan at any time.

items.question: Is there a minimum savings amount?
items.answer: Your savings target must be at least R1.00. There is no maximum limit, though it cannot exceed your available wallet balance.

items.question: How do I buy airtime or data?
items.answer: From the home screen, tap 'Mobile'. Select your network (Vodacom, MTN, CellC, or Telkom), choose a bundle, enter the recipient's mobile number, and confirm the purchase. The top-up is processed immediately.

items.question: Which networks are supported?
items.answer: We currently support Vodacom, MTN, CellC, and Telkom Mobile. More networks may be added in future.

items.question: Can I buy airtime or data for someone else?
items.answer: Yes. When entering the recipient number, simply type in any valid South African mobile number — it does not have to be your own.

items.question: How quickly does the top-up reflect?
items.answer: Mobile top-ups are processed instantly. The recipient should receive their airtime or data within a few seconds of a successful purchase.

items.question: Is there a minimum or maximum purchase amount?
items.answer: The available bundles are fixed amounts set by each network. You can only purchase the bundles listed — custom amounts are not supported at this time.

items.question: I entered the wrong number. Can I get a refund?
items.answer: Unfortunately mobile top-ups cannot be reversed once processed, as the value is delivered directly to the recipient's number. Please double-check the number before confirming your purchase.

items.question: The purchase failed. What should I do?
items.answer: Check that you have sufficient wallet balance and that the recipient number is a valid South African mobile number in 071 234 5678 or +27 71 234 5678 format. If the problem persists, contact our support team with the date and amount of the failed transaction.

items.question: Where can I see my past mobile purchases?
items.answer: Tap the clock icon in the top right corner of the Mobile page to view your full purchase history.

items.question: The app is not loading. What should I do?
items.answer: Try closing and reopening the app. If the issue persists, check your internet connection, clear the app cache, or reinstall the app. Contact support if the problem continues.

items.question: Why is my payment showing as 'pending'?
items.answer: Pending payments are usually processed within a few hours. If a payment has been pending for more than 24 hours, please contact our support team with your transaction reference number.

items.question: I'm getting an error during verification. What now?
items.answer: Ensure you are in good lighting for the selfie step and that your ID details match exactly. If verification continues to fail, contact support and we'll assist you manually.
